identity_notes.summary: Synthetic Birch continuity example for a Lambda Lang Continuity-Cost Protocol (CCP) editing session. The host agent reconciles PADCN Certainty with Birch-style orientation vs. execution phases using a disk-backed lambda-memory.md narrative scaffold that is fully injected at session start.

denominators.label: 0-30 minutes from t0 (synthetic example window)
denominators.notes: Illustrative early CCP editing band capturing orientation around PADCN Certainty and Birch continuity framing.

denominators.label: First quartile of session events
denominators.notes: Early events when the agent is still re-deriving the CCP framing and aligning its PADCN axes with Birch metrics.

denominators.label: Fourth quartile of session events
denominators.notes: Late events dominated by concrete edits to CCP text with almost no residual orientation.

denominator_metrics.notes: Synthetic early band where the agent spends several turns reconciling CCP goals, PADCN Certainty, and Birch-style orientation vs. execution before committing to irreversible edits.
First quartile mixes exploratory CCP structuring with a few early structural edits; orientation events cluster here.
Tail of the session is execution-heavy: consolidating CCP sections and tightening definitions with effectively no remaining orientation.
